name: EGFRm-002
rationale: 1,6-Naphthyridine-3-carboxamide scaffold engages Met793 via amide NH/carbonyl, chloro/methoxy substitution orients into Met790 pocket, and methylpiperazine provides solubility.
expected_advantages: Distinct scaffold from gefitinib/osimertinib chemotypes
Amide carbonyl acts as H-bond acceptor toward hinge backbone NH
Chloro substituent provides steric selectivity for T790M over WT
Methylpiperazine ensures aqueous solubility and oral bioavailability

name: EGFRm-003
rationale: Pyrido[2,3-d]pyrimidinone scaffold with N-aryl bearing ortho-trifluoromethyl group directed at Met790 hydrophobic pocket and an acetylpiperazine for solvent front interactions.
expected_advantages: CF3 group fills Met790 enlarged hydrophobic cavity selectively
Lactam carbonyl provides additional H-bond with Asp855
Acetylpiperazine reduces lipophilicity and improves ADME
Fluorophenyl enhances membrane permeability while maintaining MW

name: EGFRm-004
rationale: Pyrrolopyrimidine (7-azaindole-fused) hinge anchor with CF3-phenyl amine exploiting Met790 bulk selectivity and morpholine front pocket occupancy.
expected_advantages: 7H-pyrrolo[2,3-d]pyrimidine NH donates to Met793 hinge acceptor
CF3 phenyl selectivity for bulkier T790M gatekeeper cavity
Morpholine at C5 improves solubility and reduces LogP
Simple two-step convergent synthesis feasible

name: EGFRm-006
rationale: Uracil-based pyrimidinedione scaffold where two carbonyls can engage Met793 hinge and Asp855, with N-benzyl bearing ortho-fluorine directed toward Met790 and acetylpiperazine for solubility.
expected_advantages: Dual carbonyl oxygens engage both hinge NH and Asp855 simultaneously
Ortho-fluorobenzyl provides selectivity for Met790 vs smaller Thr790
Novel pyrimidinedione chemotype with no overlap with approved EGFR drugs
Acetylpiperazine ensures TPSA and solubility compliance

name: EGFRm-007
rationale: Quinoline-based (not quinazoline) scaffold with dimethoxy substitution mimicking DFG-region contacts and piperidine-phenyl amine providing hinge binding and solubility.
expected_advantages: Quinoline N engages Met793 NH directly
Dimethoxyphenyl occupies adenine pocket with selectivity
Free piperidine NH provides solubility and H-bond capacity
Non-quinazoline scaffold avoids first-gen TKI structural class

name: EGFRm-008
rationale: 2-Aryl-4-quinolinone scaffold where lactam NH and C4-carbonyl bind Met793 hinge, tert-butyl amine fills Met790 bulky pocket selectively, and morpholinyl phenyl occupies solvent front.
expected_advantages: Quinolinone lactam provides dual H-bond donor/acceptor for hinge
Tert-butyl group creates steric selectivity for T790M gatekeeper
Morpholine phenyl at C2 improves aqueous solubility
Distinct 4-quinolinone chemotype absent from approved EGFR inhibitor list

name: EGFRm-009
rationale: Benzimidazole hinge scaffold with C5-benzamide directing methylpiperazine to solvent front and N-methyl group providing metabolic stability; benzimidazole N3 accepts H-bond from Met793 NH.
expected_advantages: Benzimidazole N3 is efficient H-bond acceptor from Met793
Amide linker provides conformational rigidity toward DFG region
Methylpiperazine improves solubility and oral bioavailability
Compact MW allows addition of selectivity groups within limit

name: EGFRm-010
rationale: Benzoxazinone-derived scaffold with N-aryl 2-fluoro-4-chloro substitution fitting Met790 enlarged pocket and morpholine for front pocket solubilization.
expected_advantages: Chloro at para and fluoro at ortho create selectivity for Met790M vs WT Thr
Isopropyl on oxazinone enhances hydrophobic contact depth
Morpholine maintains favorable TPSA and solubility
Novel 1,3-benzoxazin-4-one chemotype unexplored in EGFR TKI space

name: EGFRm-012
rationale: Imidazo[4,5-b]pyridine scaffold exploiting the bicyclic hinge-binding motif with C6 acetylpiperazine for front pocket and 2-fluoro-4-chloroaniline filling Met790 bulky space.
expected_advantages: Imidazopyridine N1-H donates to Met793 backbone and N3 accepts from Met793 NH
2F-4Cl aniline provides dual halogen-based selectivity for T790M over WT
Acetylpiperazine lowers LogP and improves metabolic stability
Methyl on imidazole reduces CYP3A4-mediated oxidation

name: EGFRm-014
rationale: 2-Aryl-1H-indazole scaffold where indazole NH engages Met793 hinge, C5-methylpiperazine occupies solvent front, and alpha-fluoropivaloyl amide adds bulk toward Met790 with metabolic stability.
expected_advantages: Indazole NH is a well-validated Met793 hinge H-bond donor
Alpha-fluoro neopentyl amide fills Met790M enlarged cavity selectively
Methylpiperazine ensures solubility and bioavailability
Distinct indazole-aryl chemotype not present in approved T790M inhibitors
